Article Title: IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad sign Gerald Coetzee as David Payne replacement
Article Text:
Gerald Coetzee (AFP Photo)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

NEW DELHI: Sunrisers Hyderabad have signed Gerald Coetzee as a replacement for David Payne for the remainder of IPL 2026. SRH confirmed the development in an official media advisory on Saturday, with Payne ruled out due to an ankle injury. The left-arm pacer played two matches this season and picked up two wickets before being sidelined."Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) have signed Gerald Coetzee as a replacement for David Payne for the remainder of TATA Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026. Payne - who played 2 games for SRH in the ongoing IPL season and picked 2 wickets from the same - is ruled out of the remainder of the season due to an ankle injury," the league said in a statement.Coetzee, a right-arm fast bowler, brings valuable international experience, having represented South Africa national cricket team across formats. The 24-year-old has featured in four Tests, 14 ODIs and 18 T20Is, taking a combined 67 wickets.He had gone unsold in this year’s auction but has prior IPL experience with Mumbai Indians and Gujarat Titans, claiming 15 wickets in 14 matches.The 25-year-old was part of South Africa’s squad for the T20 World Cup but did not get a game.After the tournament, he played all five T20Is against New Zealand. He will join SRH for INR 2 crore.Meanwhile, SRH have also been boosted by the return of captain Pat Cummins, who linked up with the squad on April 17 and is expected to feature in their April 25 clash against Rajasthan Royals. Currently placed fifth on the table, SRH are set to face Chennai Super Kings on April 18.Coetzee, known for his pace and aggressive style, adds further strength to SRH’s bowling unit as they push for a strong finish to the season. He joins the Hyderabad-based franchise for INR 2 crore as a like-for-like pace replacement following Payne’s injury setback.
Article Summary: Gerald Coetzee joins Sunrisers Hyderabad as a replacement for David Payne. Payne has been ruled out of the remainder of IPL 2026 with an ankle injury. The left- arm pacer played two matches this season and picked up two wickets.

Article Title: IPL 2026: RR manager Romi Bhinder fined Rs 1 lakh for phone use in dugout
Article Text:
RR manager Romi Bhinder fined(Screengrab)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Items banned in IPL dugout:

MUMBAI : Rajasthan Royals manager Romi Bhinder has been fined Rs 1 lakh and issued a warning by the BCCI for using a mobile phone in the dugout during his team’s match against RCB in Guwahati on April 10. The punishment has been treated as a first-time offence under the IPL ’s PMOA (Prevention of Matchfixing, Anti-Corruption and Security) protocol, with no further action taken. As per rules, team officials are allowed to use mobile phones in the dressing room but not in the dugout.Bhinder, who has been associated with the Royals since the inaugural season in 2008, breached protocol by using his phone outside the designated area. “We noticed photographs of him using a mobile phone in the dugout. On April 12, the ACU issued him a notice under Article 4.1.1 of the BCCI PMOA protocols, asking for a reply within 48 hours. While he responded within the stipulated time, we were not satisfied with his explanation,” BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia told TOI.“Since this is a first-time offence, a fine of Rs 1 lakh has been imposed under clause 7.42 (a), along with a warning to strictly adhere to the protocols going forward,” he added.Mobile phones & personal electronic devices, smart watches and communication wearables, laptops and tablets (team analysts exempted), non-approved staff, unauthorized recording equipment (cameras, mics, scanners), alcohol or tobacco products, non‑approved team communications equipment (walkie-talkies allowed for player support personnel for communication), betting materials/gambling-related items, unauthorized team apparel/commercial merchandise, weapons/ hazardous objects.
Article Summary: Rajasthan Royals manager Romi Bhinder has been fined Rs 1 lakh and issued a warning by the BCCI for using a mobile phone in the dugout. The punishment has been treated as a first-time offence under the IPL ’s PMOA (Prevention of Matchfixing, Anti-Corruption and Security) protocol, with no further action taken.

Article Title: MI season in Jeopardy after fourth straight loss, Jayawardene flags bowling concerns
Article Text:
Jayawardene (left) with Mhambrey and Tendulkar (Image: IPL)

Watch Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula

MI cautious over Rohit’s return

MUMBAI : Mahela Jayawardene did his best to defend his side, but deep down the Mumbai Indians coach knows the season is slipping away rapidly. Defeat to Punjab Kings was MI’s fourth on the trot, a result Jayawardene attributed to poor bowling at crucial moments. On a surface where the PBKS bowlers extracted movement with the new ball, MI were put on the back foot immediately after Deepak Chahar’s opening over went for 21 runs.“We lost momentum at the back end while batting and then didn’t set the tone with the ball. That first over hurt us a lot,” Jayawardene said, “If we had controlled that phase, especially the first 10 overs before the dew set in and taken a few wickets, it could have been a different game.One of the pillars of MI’s titlewinning campaigns has been their ability to dominate the Powerplay with the ball. That has deserted them this season. They conceded 78/1 against KKR, restricted Delhi Capitals to 42/2 before losing control in the middle overs, leaked 59 in a shortened Powerplay (3.2 overs) against Rajasthan Royals, gave away 71 runs to RCB and were taken for 62/2 by PBKS on Thursday.Jasprit Bumrah, meanwhile, has gone wicketless for five matches in a row, though Jayawardene was quick to defend his spearhead.“Bumrah is bowling well. We’re just not putting pressure in the Powerplay. Batters know they don’t need to take too many risks against him. As a unit, we’ve lacked penetration across surfaces.”He added that Bumrah has also been short on luck. “If someone else is taking wickets, Bumrah gets match-ups he’s very comfortable with. He bowled some good balls to Shreyas initially, but didn’t get rewards. Once he starts taking wickets, he could be unstoppable.”Despite a respectable economy rate of 8.63, Bumrah’s impact has been blunted by the lack of support from the other pacers. Hardik Pandya faces a tactical dilemma in maximising Bumrah’s 24 balls. The rightarm pacer conceded just 14 runs in two Powerplay overs, but by the time he returned in the 13th, Shreyas Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh were well set, and Bumrah went for 16 on a flat deck. Pandya (ER 11.16), Trent Boult (12.22), Deepak Chahar (13.38) and Shardul Thakur (13.57) have all struggled to contain runs.Given Rohit Sharma ’s history of hamstring injuries, MI are taking a cautious approach. “Ro started running yesterday,” Jayawardene said. “We’ll take it day by day. It’s nothing serious, but it’s early in the season, and we don’t want to push him,” said the Sri Lankan.
Article Summary: MI coach Mahela Jayawardene blames poor bowling at crucial moments. Defeat to Punjab Kings was MI’s fourth on the trot. Jasprit Bumrah has gone wicketless for five matches in a row. Hardik Pandya faces a tactical dilemma in maximising his 24 balls.

Article Title: SRH’s new heroes take on Chennai Super Kings in high-stakes derby
Article Text:
SRH vs CSK (BCCI/IPL Photo)

Watch Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula

HYDERABAD: The morale in Sunrisers Hyderabad’s dugout is high ahead of their clash against Chennai Super Kings at the Rajiv Gandhi International stadium on Saturday. Riding on sensational debuts of their pacers Praful Hinge and Sakib Hussain, the former champions tamed high-flying Rajasthan Royals by 57 runs to return to winning ways. That victory should hold them in good stead ahead of the southern derby.SRH’s bowling, in the absence of Pat Cummins, was considered the weak link. And the lacklustre performances of the experienced duo of Jaydev Unadkat and Harshal Patel forced the think-tank to go with rookies Hinge and Hussain. And they delivered in style. While Hinge (4/34) picked up three wickets, including Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel and Lhuan-dre Pretorius, in the very first over to enter the record books, Hussain returned an impressive 4/24 to become overnight sensations. What also worked in their favour was that their spin department of Shivang Kumar and Harsh Dubey is slowly finding rhythm.The batting, however, continues to be the hosts’ forte. Travishek, Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma, though struggling for consistency, can take apart any bowling attack on their day. Both of them added 105 runs in the powerplay against Punjab Kings. But Abhishek is yet to score a run at home after for consecutive ducks against LSG and RR.On the other hand, Super Kings, who began with three defeats, recorded back-to-back wins with all-round performances. The five-time champions defeated Delhi Capitals and Kolkata Knight Riders to find some momentum.While the form of their captain Ruturaj Gaikwad still a concern, Sanju Samson finally announced his arrival in the yellow with a century and a masterful 48 in the last two matches. Ayush Mhatre too is in fine nick while Sarfaraz Khan, Dewald Brevis and Shivam Dube add muscle.Their bowling unit suffered a blow as Khaleel Ahmed was ruled out of the tournament. Though not big names, Anshul Kamboj, Gurjapneet Singh, Jamie Overton in the pace department, Noor Ahmed’s return to form and the presence of Akeal Hosein in the spin department makes it a versatile attack.Both teams have four points with two wins each and with proven power-hitters in both sides, the bowling units hold the key to the outcome.
Article Summary: SRH's bowling, in the absence of Pat Cummins, was considered the weak link. Praful Hinge (4/34) picked up three wickets, including Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Dhruv Jurel and Lhuan-dre Pretorius. Hussain returned an impressive 4/24 to become overnight sensations.

Article Title: Ponting’s advice, Siraj’s trick help Arshdeep rediscover rhythm
Article Text:
Arshdeep Singh (PTI Photo)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

MUMBAI : “Going back to the basics,” may be the most cliched line delivered by a player but when the mind is muddled, it is often the most effective remedy. Arshdeep Singh also went down that road, found his answers and delivered a Player-of-the-Match performance in Punjab Kings’ seven-wicket win over Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday.The 27-year-old came into the MI match short on confidence and with his line and length all over the place. He had taken just two wickets in four matches and in the Powerplay, he had gone wicketless in 10 games stretching back to last season.But against MI, the outswinger was back while the tailing yorkers were on the money. Arshdeep finished with 3/22 from four overs, accounting for Ryan Rickelton, Suryakumar Yadav and Sherfane Rutherford and became the 31st bowler to cross 100 IPL -wickets.In his second over, he revealed his new variation, a scrambled seam delivery that tailed in, and Rickelton, cramped for room, hit it to backward square leg. The very next ball, a classic outswinger caught Surya off guard as he edged it to short third man. The left-arm pacer returned in the death overs to land a perfect yorker to rattle Rutherford’s stumps, reminding everyone that his full repertoire remains intact.In the previous four matches, Arshdeep had conceded 16 wides, which mostly came in bunches in his endeavour to stay away from batters’ arc.Against MI, though, the accuracy was back as he bowled just one wide. Later, Arshdeep attributed his effort to clearing the clutter to a pep talk from coach Ricky Ponting.“I told Ricky that the ball is not coming out of my hand well. He just told me to keep myself fresh mentally and not expect too much out of myself and just go with the flow,” he said.“He said, ‘You’re coming off a high winning a World Cup and it’s not easy to maintain that high all the time. So you’ve just got to stick to the basics and have fun. That’s the most important part of the game. Just enjoy it and stick to your basics,’” he added.The Rickelton wicket came from a scrambled seam delivery, which he has been working on after seeing the success India teammate Mohammed Siraj achieved with it. “That is the ball I’ve been trying to work on. Siraj got a lot of wickets with the scrambled seam. It’s still not there yet, but I’m trying to learn it as much as I can as a variation. I just wanted to change it a bit,” Arshdeep explained.PBKS’s spin-bowling coach Sairaj Bahutule explained how hard Arshdeep worked in the nets to regain his groove and confidence. “You encounter different conditions at different venues and expectations are high. Sometimes you forget to bowl your stock ball. But he’s very self-aware, went back to his basics, did a lot of spot bowling and came back with the understanding that he must focus on what he does best. And that’s what he did in this game,” said Bahutule.
Article Summary: Arshdeep Singh was Player-of-the-Match in Punjab Kings' seven-wicket win over Mumbai Indians. The 27-year-old came into the MI match short on confidence and with his line and length all over the place. Arshdeep finished with 3/22 from four overs, accounting for Ryan Rickelton, Suryakumar Yadav and Sherfane Rutherford.

Article Title: Gill's composed 86 helps team chase down 181; Green’s 79 goes in vain as victory eludes knights again
Article Text:
KKR and GT players after the match in Ahmedabad. (Pic credit: IPL)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

AHMEDABAD: Often bracketed among batters whose strike rates don’t align with the demands of modern-day T20 batting, Gujarat Titans captain Shubman Gill (86 off 50b; 8x4, 4x6) showed his ball-striking abilities to notch up a third consecutive half-century and help the hosts seal a five-wicket triumph over winless Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) at the Narendra Modi Stadium here.The pursuit of 181 began in a commanding fashion for the Titans, who raced to 71/1 by the end of the Powerplay. Gill and B Sai Sudharsan (22) went after left-arm spinner Anukul Roy in his first over, smashing three sixes, to put the Titans chase on track. Despite the loss of his fellow opener, Gill and Jos Buttler (25) took Gujarat to 93/1 after eight overs.KKR applied the squeeze, forcing Buttler to hole out to Varun Chakaravarthy (2/34), while Washington Sundar (13) failed to get going. Despite Gill’s dismissal to an outstanding diving catch by Cameron Green giving KKR a glimmer of hope to pull off an upset, Glenn Phillips (19) and the rest of the batters took the Titans home with a ball to spare.\Prior to this, Green reminded everyone of his white-ball pedigree with a composed yet forceful 79 off 55 balls, studded with seven fours and four sixes. Walking in with KKR in early trouble, the Australian allrounder first absorbed pressure and then took the attack back to Gujarat Titans, dragging his side to 180. The score was competitive but not quite what it could have been. Only three other KKR batters reached double figures — Tim Seifert (19), Rovman Powell (27) and Ramandeep Singh (17) — underlining how solitary Green’s effort really was.KKR’s decision to bat first misfired almost immediately. Ajinkya Rahane fell first ball, caught by Kagiso Rabada off Mohammed Siraj. Angkrish Raghuvanshi soon followed for eight as Gujarat’s new-ball pair tightened the screws.Seifert threat ened briefly with a couple of crisp boundaries but his 19 ended when he picked out Glenn Phillips off Rabada, leaving KKR 37/3 at the end of the Powerplay.Siraj was superb when it came to his control and discipline, returning 2/23, while Rabada’s heavy lengths and late movement earned him 3/29 and complete command of the opening phase of the game.The repair began when Powell joined Green. The West Indian briefly shifted momentum with two sixes in a brisk 27 off 20 before holing out to Ashok Sharma, but not before adding 55 for the fourth wicket with Green. From there, Green took over. He reached his fifty off 34 balls, then launched a decisive assault as KKR surged from 59/3 at the first strategic timeout to 147/5 by the second. Green and Anukul Roy added 60.
Article Summary: Gujarat Titans beat Kolkata Knight Riders by five wickets in IPL. Shubman Gill top scored with 86 off 50 balls for the hosts. Cameron Green top scored for KKR with 79 off 55 balls. Tim Seifert (19), Rovman Powell (27) and Ramandeep Singh (17) all made double figures.

Article Title: 'Last year, I played...': Moeen Ali opens up on leaving IPL for PSL opportunity
Article Text:
Moeen Ali in PSL (Screengrab/X)

Watch Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula

Karachi Kings Move: A Fresh Chapter

Former England all-rounder Moeen Ali has opted to feature in the 2026 Pakistan Super League instead of returning to the Indian Premier League , underlining a growing shift among overseas players seeking more game time and stability in the latter stages of their careers. With the IPL and PSL schedules clashing in recent seasons, several international cricketers, especially those uncertain of regular selection in IPL franchises, have started exploring opportunities in Pakistan’s premier T20 competition.At 38, Moeen made it clear that his priority is simple: to stay relevant and enjoy cricket while he still can.“I'm 38 now, and I still want to play, do well, and extend my career as much as possible… It’s a shorter competition, which works better for my family and me,” Moeen said on the ARY Podcast.The England veteran had a difficult IPL 2025 campaign with Kolkata Knight Riders, where he managed just 5 runs in six matches and picked up six wickets, eventually losing his place in the playing XI. Reflecting on that phase, Moeen highlighted the lack of opportunities as a major factor behind his decision."Last year in the IPL, I played only half the games and barely got to bat, just twice. At this stage, I want to be out there; I want to bat. I feel like I’m playing good cricket towards the back end of my career, and I just want to keep going," he added.Moeen was signed directly by Karachi Kings ahead of PSL 2026 for a reported PKR 6.44 crore, bringing his vast experience to the franchise.The move also marks his return to the PSL after previously featuring for Multan Sultans, with whom he won the title in 2021. Karachi Kings viewed Moeen as a key all-round addition, capable of strengthening both the middle order and spin department in a competitive PSL season.He has primarily played in the middle order (No. 5–7), with his standout performance being an unbeaten 48 off 29 balls in the season opener against Quetta Gladiators. He has provided crucial breakthroughs with his off-spin, notably taking 2 wickets for 31 runs against Lahore Qalandars. In his most recent match against Islamabad United on April 16, he scored 8 runs and took 1 wicket.In addition to his PSL commitments, Moeen recently reversed his domestic retirement to sign a T20 Blast-only contract with Yorkshire for the 2026 English seasonThis shift reflects a broader mindset change, players now prioritise: Regular playing time, role clarity and career longevity"You’re never guaranteed a place anywhere, even here, but I just want to enjoy my cricket as much as I can. I’ve always wanted to experience the PSL, but unfortunately, it slightly clashes with the IPL," Moeen concluded.
Article Summary: Former England all-rounder Moeen Ali has opted to feature in the 2026 Pakistan Super League instead of returning to the Indian Premier League. Moeen was signed directly by Karachi Kings ahead of PSL 2026 for a reported PKR 6.44 crore, bringing his vast experience to the franchise.

Article Title: Yesterday IPL Match Result: Shubman Gill dazzles, Gujarat Titans break into top 4 after KKR win
Article Text:
Image credit: BCCI/IPL

Watch Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula

NEW DELHI: Shubman Gill led from the front with a superb half-century as Gujarat Titans beat Kolkata Knight Riders by five wickets in Ahmedabad on Friday.Gill played a brilliant knock of 86 off 50 balls, hitting eight fours and four sixes, and controlled the chase for most of the innings. His effort helped GT comfortably chase down 181 and also handed KKR their sixth straight loss of the season, keeping them at the bottom of the table.KKR tried to fight back towards the end, but Rahul Tewatia and Shahrukh Khan kept their calm and finished the match with two balls to spare, sealing GT’s third win in a row. KKR will be disappointed as Cameron Green’s 79 went in vain due to lack of support from others.Chasing 181, GT got off to a flying start. Gill looked in great touch from the beginning, playing elegant shots all around the ground. He was well supported by Sai Sudharsan, who scored 22 and helped GT reach 50 in just over four overs.After Sudharsan got out, Jos Buttler added a quick 25 before being dismissed by Varun Chakaravarthy. Gill continued his attacking approach, reaching his fifty in just 27 balls and looking set for a century before being dismissed by a sharp effort from Green in the field.KKR made things slightly tense by taking a few late wickets. In the final over, they needed five runs to win. Ramandeep Singh gave KKR hope by dismissing Glenn Phillips on the first ball. However, Tewatia and Shahrukh held their nerve to guide GT home.
Article Summary: Gujarat Titans beat Kolkata Knight Riders by five wickets in Ahmedabad. Shubman Gill scored 86 off 50 balls, hitting eight fours and four sixes. Rahul Tewatia and Shahrukh Khan finished the match with two balls to spare.

Article Title: Shubman Gill's big IPL title warning after Gujarat Titans beat Kolkata Knight Riders
Article Text:
With six points from five matches, Gujarat Titans have climbed to fourth on the table. (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula Shreyas Iyer masterclass & Arshdeep Singh's comeback! PBKS reveal winning formula

NEW DELHI: Gujarat Titans skipper Shubman Gill wore a satisfied smile after leading his side to a thrilling five-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders in their IPL 2026 clash at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Friday. While pleased with the result, Gill admitted his team would have preferred to finish the chase earlier.The victory marked GT’s third consecutive win this season, following triumphs over Delhi Capitals and Lucknow Super Giants. With six points from five matches, the Titans have climbed to fourth on the table. In contrast, KKR’s struggles continued, as they remain winless with just one point, rooted to the bottom."Honestly, I don't think it matters. The only thing that matters is the win and obviously, playing the final and winning the cup would be something that I'm looking forward for. (after you got out, did you think it's taking longer than you wanted?) Yeah, ideally we would have liked to finish at least an over, couple of overs before and I was quite disappointed with the way I got out. I wanted to be there till the end and finish it.Hopefully, the next chance I get, I would be able to do that," Gill said."(on bowling Siraj and Rabada 3 on the trot early) Honestly, the way both of them were bowling was, it looked like they could get a wicket on every ball and giving both of them one extra over in the end, I could see the ball was nipping a little bit, so it was a decision, I asked both of them if they are ready to bowl one more because, it's a little bit hot, so it's not easy to bowl three overs on the trot," Gill added.Gill led from the front with a magnificent 86 off 50 balls, striking eight fours and four sixes as GT chased down 181 in 19.4 overs."(on his batting) Yeah, definitely. I think fitness was something, I got a couple of months before this. And fitness was one thing that I worked on. And apart from that, just working on the mental aspect of the game and trying to get better, not just technically, but tactically as well. (how do you get better technically and mentally?) It's all about, try to stay in the present, not try to think too far ahead or look into your past mistakes. Obviously, you want to learn from them, but you don't want to dwell on them. And that is something that, it's a conscious decision every time, you go to bat or you go in the field, your mind takes you to all sorts of places, but it's important to stay present," he said.
Article Summary: Gujarat Titans beat Kolkata Knight Riders by five wickets in IPL 2026 clash. Shubman Gill top scored with 86 off 50 balls, striking eight fours and four sixes. Victory marked GT’s third consecutive win this season, following triumphs over Delhi Capitals and Lucknow Super Giants.

Article Title: ISL clubs seek structured talks with AIFF, propose owners’ committee
Article Text:
ISL seek role to find AIFF’s rights partner (Screengrab)

PANAJI: Indian Super League (ISL) club owners have proposed the formation of a club working committee involving the owners and decision makers, who would then engage with the federation in a “structured and transparent” manner to find the best taker for its long-term commercial rights.In a letter signed by 13 of the 14 owners, except Inter Kashi, the clubs said the group wanted to provide an appropriate platform through which ideas can be exchanged, common ground can be identified, and a balanced, sustainable, and mutually beneficial framework can be developed together.The clubs proposed three owners, Bhavesh Jindal/ Dhruv Sood (SC Delhi), Nikhil Nimmagadda (Kerala Blasters) and Rohan Sharma (Odisha FC), in the committee, while other representatives include Ravi Puskur (CEO, FC Goa), Ekansh Gupta (VP, Chennaiyin), Darren Caldeira (CEO, Bengaluru) and Vinay Chopra (director, Mohun Bagan SG).The club owners’ letter to AIFF president Kalyan Chaubey comes two days after the federation formed the ISL Governing Council and asked remaining six clubs to submit the name of their representative latest by Friday. The AIFF’s response, interestingly, came after an online meeting of all club owners just a day earlier.The club owners had little choice but join hands since the AIFF appears keen to accept the bid from Genius Sports and hand the London-based company the commercial rights of the ISL for 15+5 years. Genius’ Rs 64.4 crore annual bid, or approx. Rs 2,130 crore over 20 years with a five percent increment per year, needs an approval from the general body.The clubs aren’t happy with the rights procees and believe it’s important to engage with all stakeholders.
Article Summary: Indian Super League (ISL) club owners have proposed the formation of a club working committee involving the owners and decision makers. The committee would then engage with the federation in a ‘structured and transparent’ manner to find the best taker for its long-term commercial rights.

Article Title: Chennaiyin beat Delhi to break home jinx; Odisha split points
Article Text:
Chennaiyin beat Delhi

CHENNAI: At long last, Chennaiyin FC gave their fans a reason to cheer, breaking their home jinx in the Indian Super League this season after beating Sporting Club Delhi here on Friday.Farukh Choudhary’s 11th-minute strike handed them a 1-0 win and took CFC to eight points from eight matches, same as Delhi’s. After a bright attacking start, Farukh, receiving a pass from Alberto Noguera on the left, advanced into the box and unleashed a shot with his weaker left foot. Goalkeeper Nora Fernandes could not do much and watched it sail over him into the top-right corner.Just before half-time, defender Alex Saji denied CFC a two-goal cushion. Irfan Yadwad delivered a teasing cross from the right that fell kindly for Daniel Chima Chukwu, but just as the striker pulled the trigger, Saji from inside the six-yard box slid in to block and keep the lead in check. After the break, the home side struggled to make inroads into the attacking third in the opening 10 minutes while Delhi kept creating opportunities.Odisha FC settle for draw as Md Sp earn first point: Odisha FC’s finishing woes continued to haunt them at home as well as they were held to a 1-1 draw by wooden spooners Mohammedan Sporting Club in the Indian Super League at Kalinga Stadium in Bhubaneswar on Friday, reports Sourav Modak.For the Kolkata giants though, it was a welcome result as they brought home a point, their first in this edition following seven straight losses. The hosts had taken the lead in the 40th minute through VP Suhair while T Adison Singh scored the equaliser for MSC in the 57th minute as the game ended with scores level at the final whistle.
Article Summary: Farukh Choudhary’s 11th-minute strike handed them a 1-0 win and took CFC to eight points from eight matches. VP Suhair took the lead for the hosts in the 40th minute while T Adison Singh scored the equaliser for MSC in the 57th minute.

Article Title: FIFA president Infantino confirms Iran’s participation in 2026 World Cup in USA
Article Text:
Iran national football team (Reuters Photo)

FIFA president Gianni Infantino has confirmed that Iran will take part in the 2026 FIFA World Cup despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East, stressing that football must remain separate from politics.Speaking at a CNBC-hosted economic conference on Wednesday, Infantino said, "Iran is coming for sure. We hope that by then the situation will be a peaceful situation, that would definitely help,""But Iran has to come, they represent their people, they have qualified, the players want to play," he added as the team's upcoming matches scheduled in the United States in June.Iran’s participation had come under uncertainty following the conflict involving the United States and Israel earlier this year. The team had even considered a boycott and requested that their matches be moved out of the United States, a proposal FIFA ultimately rejected. The Asian side is scheduled to play its Group G matches in the United States, two in Los Angeles and one in Seattle, while being based in Tucson, Arizona during the tournament.Tensions escalated after the February 28 conflict, followed by weeks of air strikes and retaliatory actions.Although a fragile truce came into effect on April 8, geopolitical strains remain, including restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz and US-imposed maritime measures involving Iranian ports.Infantino had made similar comments in March, when he attended an Iran-Costa Rica friendly in Antalya, Turkey, even though US President Donald Trump had previously suggested that Iranian players might not be "safe" in the United States.Despite the backdrop, Infantino reiterated FIFA’s stance on keeping sport above political conflicts. “Sports should be outside of politics. "If there's nobody else that believes in building bridges and keeping them intact and together, we are doing the job," he said.The 2026 World Cup, co-hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico, will be the first edition to feature 48 teams and is scheduled to begin on June 11.
Article Summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino confirms Iran will take part in the 2026 World Cup. Iran's participation had come under uncertainty following the conflict involving the United States and Israel earlier this year. The Asian side is scheduled to play its Group G matches in the U.S. during the tournament.

Article Title: Atletico Madrid: Champions League: Atletico Madrid edge Barcelona 3-2 on aggregate to enter semis
Article Text:
Julian Alvarez of Atletico de Madrid takes a free kick during the UEFA Champions League 2025/26 Quarter-Final Second Leg match between Club Atlético de Madrid and FC Barcelona at Riyadh Air Metropolitano on April 14, 2026 in Madrid, Spain. (Photo/Getty Images)

Atletico Madrid knocked Barcelona out of the Champions League despite a 2-1 defeat in the second leg of their quarter-final on Tuesday, advancing 3-2 on aggregate.Barcelona took an early lead through Lamine Yamal and Ferran Torres inside the first 24 minutes. However, Ademola Lookman scored for Atletico, which proved enough after their 2-0 win in the first leg.Managed by Diego Simeone, Atletico reached the semi-finals of the competition for the first time since 2017.Barcelona’s hopes of forcing extra time were affected late in the match when defender Eric Garcia was sent off for bringing down Alexander Sorloth as he ran towards goal.Atletico, who have not won the competition and lost the finals in 2014 and 2016 under Simeone, will now face either Arsenal or Sporting Lisbon in the semi-finals.“(We're) very happy, knocking out a great Barca side. We had a great game away... it was really hard for us at the start (of this one), but the team knew how to get back on its feet,” Atletico midfielder Koke told Movistar.Barcelona last won the Champions League in 2015, and their wait for another title continues. Midfielder Frenkie de Jong said the team is improving despite the exit.“I think we had a very good game, we gave our lives out there, we tried everything,” he said.“I feel like luck was not on our side. We have to continue -- we're on a good path, we're growing every year.”Barcelona coach Hansi Flick started with Torres and Gavi in attack, leaving Marcus Rashford and Robert Lewandowski on the bench.Barcelona began strongly, with Yamal scoring in the fourth minute after pressing Clement Lenglet into an error. Torres later doubled the lead in the 24th minute after finishing a pass from Dani Olmo.Atletico responded in the 31st minute when Marcos Llorente set up Lookman to score.Barcelona thought they had a third goal in the second half through Torres, but it was ruled out for offside.Goalkeeper Joan Garcia made a save to deny Robin Le Normand, while tensions increased as Matteo Ruggieri was left bleeding after contact with Gavi.The match turned further against Barcelona when Eric Garcia was sent off late in the game. Flick responded by pushing Ronald Araujo forward, but Atletico held on through stoppage time to secure their place in the semi-finals.
Article Summary: Atletico Madrid beat Barcelona 2-1 in Champions League quarter-final. Lamine Yamal and Ferran Torres scored for Barcelona. Ademola Lookman scored for Atletico. Atletico advance 3-2 on aggregate and will face either Arsenal or Sporting Lisbon.

Article Title: Champions League: Barcelona eye Atletico comeback
Article Text:
Barcelona's Marcus Rashford, left, celebrates after scoring his side's fourth goal with Lamine Yamal, right, and Pedri during the Spanish La Liga soccer match between Barcelona and Espanyol in Barcelona, Spain, Saturday, April 11, 2026. (AP Photo)

Watch Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game

1ST LEG SCORES

Barcelona: Marcus Rashford had his chance against Atletico Madrid, but failed to take it — or any of the next five that came along on a frustrating European night.On Tuesday the England international has another opportunity to try and inspire Barcelona to a Champions League comeback in the Spanish capital.Rashford fired blanks as Barca suffered a 2-0 quarterfinal first leg defeat, but the England international was also his team’s brightest spark against the Rojiblancos, even though the killer finish eluded him. With Raphinha out injured, Rashford has been the natural choice on Barca’s left flank.Talented teenager Lamine Yamal was double and triple marked, in the first leg, to keep him under wraps. Atletico Madrid midfielder Koke said his team must play with “personality” to stop Barcelona mounting a comeback.“It’s hugely important to beat the press they usually put on,” Koke said on Monday. “They’re a team that presses very high, that pushes their line almost up to the halfway line that suffocates you.“That’s where our personality comes in, when it’s time to play, to want the ball, to make ourselves available, to make runs in behind.”Barcelona 0 vs Atletico Madrid 2 PSG 2 vs Liverpool 0
Article Summary: Rashford fired blanks as Barca suffered a 2-0 quarterfinal first leg defeat. England international was also his team’s brightest spark against the Rojiblancos. With Raphinha out injured, Rashford has been the natural choice on Barca's left flank.Talented teenager Lamine Yamal was double and triple marked, in the first leg, to keep him under wraps.

Article Title: Footballer dies after robbers attack team bus
Article Text:
Football (Representational Photo)

Watch EXCLUSIVE: Where did things go wrong for India in Women's Asia Cup? Aditi Chauhan decodes EXCLUSIVE: Where did things go wrong for India in Women's Asia Cup? Aditi Chauhan decodes

Poll Do you believe community support could help prevent such tragic incidents? Yes, community vigilance is crucial Not necessarily, crime happens everywhere

NEW DELHI: A Ghanaian Premier League footballer, Dominic Frimpong, has died after armed robbers attacked his team while they were returning from an away match on Sunday, according to the Ghana Football Association.The incident shocked the country’s football community and left his club and teammates devastated.The club, Berekum Chelsea FC, said in a statement that "masked men wielding guns and assault rifles" fired on the team bus and forced the players to take cover in the bushes.The attack reportedly happened around 10:30 pm local time along the Ahyiresu-Kwame Dwumor Sreso road in the Nyinahin District of Ghana’s Ashanti Region.During the chaos, Dominic Frimpong was hit by gunfire. Police said he was rushed to hospital but later died while receiving treatment.The violent robbery also affected others, including a 52-year-old victim. "Another victim, George Owusu Afriyie, 52, was also robbed of 4,500.00 cedis ($408)," the police said in a statement.Authorities confirmed the incident and described it as a criminal attack that led to loss of life and theft.The Ghana Football Association called the event "tragic" and a "huge loss to Berekum Chelsea but also to Ghana football as a whole."Frimpong, just 20 years old, was seen as a rising talent in Ghanaian football."Dominic was a promising young talent whose dedication and passion for the game embodied the spirit of our league," it said in a statement.He had joined Berekum Chelsea FC on loan from Aduana Stars FC earlier this year and had already played 13 matches, scoring two goals.His sudden death has left the local football community mourning a young career cut short in a violent and unexpected tragedy.
Article Summary: Ghanaian Premier League footballer, Dominic Frimpong, has died after armed robbers attacked his team. The club, Berekum Chelsea FC, said in a statement that "masked men wielding guns and assault rifles" fired on the team bus and forced the players to take cover in the bushes. Police said he was rushed to hospital but later died while receiving treatment.

Article Title: Manchester City rout Chelsea to close gap on leaders Arsenal
Article Text:
PTI Photo

- Cherki sparkles -

Manchester City ignited the Premier League title race with a second-half blitz that sealed a 3-0 win against Chelsea as they closed the gap on leaders Arsenal on Sunday. Pep Guardiola's side produced a devastating spell immediately after half-time, with Nico O'Reilly and Marc Guehi scoring in the space of six minutes at Stamford Bridge.Jeremy Doku wrapped up a statement victory to ensure City took advantage of Arsenal's shock 2-1 home defeat against Bournemouth on Saturday.Second-placed City are now only six points behind Arsenal with a game in hand, setting up a seismic showdown against the Gunners at the Etihad Stadium on April 19.Having beaten Arsenal in the League Cup final and thrashed Liverpool in the FA Cup quarter-finals, City are hitting form at just the right time.Their bid for a seventh English title under Guardiola appeared to be fading after successive draws with strugglers West Ham and Nottingham Forest in their previous two league matches.A disjointed first half against Chelsea suggested City might waste a golden opportunity to pile pressure on Arsenal.But Guardiola's half-time team-talk had the desired effect and City can now target next weekend's do-or-die visit from Mikel Arteta's spluttering team.If City win that blockbuster clash, they will be within three points of Arsenal, who blew substantial leads in the 2023 and 2024 title races, allowing Guardiola's men to pip them to the trophy.Arteta said the defeat to Bournemouth was a "punch in the face" and City's success in west London was another body blow for the Spaniard.City have won 29 of their 32 league games in the month of April in recent seasons, underlining their ability to thrive when the title pressure mounts.After winning their first four league matches following Liam Rosenior's arrival from Strasbourg to replace Enzo Maresca, Chelsea have won just one of their last seven, losing three in a row to leave the Blues boss facing some difficult questions.Chelsea, who haven't beaten City since the 2021 Champions League final, were again without Enzo Fernandez after Rosenior's controversial decision to drop the Argentine midfielder for hinting he might leave in the summer.Sixth-placed Chelsea looked subdued without Fernandez and they trail four points behind Liverpool in the race to qualify for the Champions League via a top-five finish.Lacking energy and cohesion in a sloppy start, City were fortunate not to fall behind when Marc Cucurella's clinical finish was disallowed for a tight offside.City also let Pedro Neto in far too easily for a stinging strike that forced Gianluigi Donnarumma to save at his near post.It took City 35 minutes to mount an incisive raid as Bernardo Silva stretched to meet O'Reilly's cross, but Robert Sanchez made a fine save from the midfielder's close-range effort.But City stepped up after the interval and O'Reilly made the breakthrough in the 51st minute.Rayan Cherki whipped an in-swinging cross towards O'Reilly and the City left-back reprised his League Cup final heroics with another clinical header from close range.Six minutes later, Cherki again showed the creative genius that has won over Guardiola despite some impetuous moments in his first season in Manchester.The France playmaker glided past a gaggle of Chelsea players on the edge of the area before threading a sublime pass to Guehi, who looked more like a forward than a centre-back as he smashed a perfect strike into the far corner from 12 yards.City benefitted from wretched Chelsea defending for their third goal in the 68th minute.Sanchez rolled the ball to Moises Caicedo even though the Chelsea midfielder was surrounded by three City players and Doku pounced, racing into the area to drill home as Guardiola celebrated a priceless result.
Article Summary: Manchester City beat Chelsea 3-0 at Stamford Bridge. Nico O'Reilly, Marc Guehi and Jeremy Doku on target. Second-placed City now six points behind Arsenal with a game in hand. Pep Guardiola's side face Gunners at the Etihad Stadium on April 19.

Article Title: Bournemouth stun Arsenal 2-1 at the Emirates to dent Premier League title push
Article Text:
Bournemouth's Evanilson, and Eli Junior Kroupi celebrate (AP Photo/Dave Shopland)

Arsenal’s Premier League title hopes suffered a major setback on Saturday as they were beaten 2-1 at the Emirates by Bournemouth, handing Manchester City renewed belief in the race at the top of the table.Bournemouth struck first in the 17th minute when Junior Kroupi pounced on a loose ball inside the box after a looping deflection off William Saliba fell kindly into his path, allowing him to tap home from close range and silence the home crowd.Arsenal responded and gradually grew into the game, with their pressure finally telling in the 35th minute. Viktor Gyökeres stepped up and smashed home a penalty after Ryan Christie was adjudged to have handled the ball inside the area. That goal brought Arsenal level and briefly settled nerves around the stadium.The Gunners came out stronger after the break, and Mikel Arteta reacted with bold attacking changes, introducing Eberechi Eze, 16-year-old Max Dowman and Leandro Trossard in place of Kai Havertz, Noni Madueke and Gabriel Martinelli. However, despite the fresh legs, Arsenal continued to struggle to break down Bournemouth’s disciplined defensive structure.Bournemouth, however, remained dangerous in transition and regained the lead in the 74th minute with a well-worked move. David Brooks created the opening before Evanilson’s involvement helped the ball fall to Alex Scott , who finished emphatically, drilling a powerful shot past David Raya.Arsenal threw everything forward in the closing stages but lacked precision in the final third. Gyökeres came closest late on, firing wide deep into stoppage time, as Bournemouth held firm under pressure.The hosts ended with just three shots on target and suffered only their second home league defeat of the season, a worrying sign given the timing of the run-in.Arsenal remain nine points clear at the top with six games left in their pursuit of a first Premier League title since 2004, but Manchester City, with games in hand, now have a clear opportunity to cut the gap and pile pressure on the leaders ahead of their upcoming meeting at the Etihad.
Article Summary: Bournemouth struck first in the 17th minute when Junior Kroupi pounced on a loose ball inside the box. Arsenal responded and gradually grew into the game, with their pressure finally telling in the 35th minute. Viktor Gyökeres stepped up and smashed home a penalty after Ryan Christie was adjudged to have handled the ball.

Article Title: Ollie Watkins helps Aston Villa defeat Bologna in quarter-final of Europa League
Article Text:
Aston Villa beat Bologna (Screengrab)

Aston Villa moved a step closer to the Europa League semi-finals with a hard-fought 3-1 victory over Bologna on Thursday night, in a contest that could have taken a very different turn had it not been for the clinical finishing of Ollie Watkins.Villa’s win was built on key moments at either end of the match. Ezri Konsa gave the visitors the lead just before half-time, while Watkins struck twice, once early in the second half and again deep into stoppage time, to put Unai Emery’s side firmly in control of the tie. The result leaves Villa strong favourites to progress to the last four, where they will face either FC Porto or Nottingham Forest, provided they complete the job in the second leg at Villa Park. Despite the defeat, Bologna remain in contention thanks to a late goal from Jonathan Rowe.Bologna entered the match in excellent form, having won their last seven away games in all competitions, and they began with intent. The hosts came agonisingly close to taking the lead on multiple occasions, striking the woodwork twice during a dominant first-half spell. They even thought they had gone ahead in the 26th minute when a deflection involving Konsa resulted in the ball finding the net.However, celebrations were cut short after VAR ruled that Santiago Castro had been marginally offside in the build-up.Moments later, Bologna continued to threaten, with Rowe’s energetic run down the left setting up Lewis Ferguson, whose effort crashed against the underside of the crossbar.But just as Bologna looked the more dangerous side, Villa struck a decisive blow. On the stroke of half-time, Konsa rose strongly in the box, holding off Jhon Lucumí before heading home from a corner delivered by Youri Tielemans. The goal, aided by a misjudgement from goalkeeper Federico Ravaglia, shifted the momentum firmly in Villa’s favour.Villa capitalised on that momentum early in the second half. Just five minutes after the restart, Watkins doubled the lead after Bologna defender Torbjorn Heggem carelessly gave away possession inside his own penalty area. The England striker remained composed, slotting the ball through Ravaglia’s legs to make it 2-0.Despite the setback, Bologna refused to back down. Federico Bernardeschi came close to pulling one back, striking the outside of the post as the hosts continued to press forward. Their persistence was finally rewarded in the 90th minute when Rowe produced a fine finish to give Bologna a lifeline and spark hopes of a comeback in the second leg.However, any momentum the hosts gained was quickly extinguished. Deep into stoppage time, Villa struck again from a set piece, with Watkins left completely unmarked to tap home his second goal of the night and restore the two-goal cushion.Speaking after the match, Watkins highlighted the importance of timing in Villa’s goals. “Just before half-time is a great time to score. We got the second after half-time and then felt we could always score more,” he said. “At this stage of the season, I’m raring to go. I feel like I could play another 90 minutes, I’m hungry for what’s next.”Villa’s clinical edge ultimately proved to be the difference. While Bologna created chances and showed attacking intent, their inability to convert, and costly defensive errors, allowed Emery’s experienced side to take control of the tie. With the second leg set to be played in Birmingham, Villa now have one foot in the semi-finals and are firmly on course to chase their first major trophy in three decades, as well as strengthen their push for a top-five finish in the Premier League
Article Summary: Aston Villa beat Bologna 3-1 in their Europa League quarter-final first leg. Ezri Konsa, Ollie Watkins and Jonathan Rowe scored for the visitors. Villa will now face either FC Porto or Nottingham Forest in the last four.

Article Title: Atletico Madrid beat 10-man Barcelona 2-0 to take control of quarter-final
Article Text:
Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez, right, takes a free kick during the Champions League quarterfinal first leg soccer match between Barcelona and Atletico Madrid in Barcelona, Spain, Wednesday, April 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Joan Monfort)

Atletico Madrid moved a step closer to the Champions League semi-finals with a commanding 2-0 victory over Barcelona in the first leg of their quarter-final clash at Camp Nou. Goals from Julian Alvarez and Alexander Sorloth, combined with a pivotal red card for Barcelona, gave Diego Simeone’s side a significant advantage heading into the return leg.Barcelona, currently leading La Liga, began the match on the front foot and looked the more composed side in the early stages of this all-Spanish encounter. However, the game took a dramatic turn when Pau Cubarsi was dismissed after bringing down Giuliano Simeone, who was through on goal. Although the referee initially showed a yellow card, VAR intervention upgraded it to a red, leaving the hosts with 10 men before half-time.Atletico capitalised immediately. Alvarez stepped up to take the resulting free-kick and delivered a stunning strike into the top corner, putting the visitors ahead. The goal shifted the momentum firmly in Atletico’s favour, even though Barcelona continued to show resilience despite being a man down.In the second half, Barcelona pushed hard to find an equaliser. Marcus Rashford, who has been in lively form, posed a constant threat down the left flank and created multiple opportunities.However, Atletico’s defence held firm, and goalkeeper Juan Musso made crucial interventions when needed.Against the run of play, Atletico struck again. A well-worked attacking move involving Antoine Griezmann and Matteo Ruggieri ended with a precise cross into the box, where Sorloth used his physical presence to outmuscle his marker and finish confidently past the goalkeeper, doubling Atletico’s lead with around 20 minutes remaining.Barcelona’s players and coaching staff were left frustrated not only by the result but also by certain officiating decisions. Head coach Hansi Flick expressed dissatisfaction with VAR, particularly questioning why Cubarsi’s challenge was reviewed while a possible handball incident involving Atletico defender Marc Pubill was not examined.Despite their numerical disadvantage, Barcelona continued to fight and created several chances, with Rashford coming close on multiple occasions. However, they were unable to find the breakthrough and ultimately suffered their first home defeat since Camp Nou reopened in November 2025.For Atletico Madrid, the result carries added significance. It marks Diego Simeone’s first-ever win at Camp Nou in his 14-year tenure and the club’s first victory at the stadium since 2006. Having already eliminated Barcelona from the Copa del Rey semi-finals earlier this year, Atletico now hold a strong psychological and tactical edge.Speaking after the match, Antoine Griezmann urged caution despite the positive result, emphasising that the tie is far from over and that the team must remain focused ahead of the second leg.Barcelona, who last lifted the Champions League trophy in 2015, now face a daunting task in the return leg in Madrid. They will need a remarkable comeback to overturn the deficit and keep their European ambitions alive.Meanwhile, Atletico Madrid, two-time finalists in 2014 and 2016, are now in a strong position as they continue their pursuit of a first-ever Champions League title.
Article Summary: Atletico Madrid beat Barcelona 2-0 in Champions League quarter-final first leg. Goals from Julian Alvarez and Alexander Sorloth gave Diego Simeone's side a significant advantage heading into the return leg. Barcelona's Pau Cubarsi was sent off after bringing down Giuliano Sime one.

Article Title: Dianna Russini And Mike Vrabel: “Neither could prove”: Dianna Russini under fire as report reveals alleged coordination with Mike Vrabel after Page Six reached out for evidence
Article Text:
Dianna Russini under fire as report reveals alleged coordination with Mike Vrabel after Page Six reached out for evidence(Getty Images)

Fresh report points to coordinated response from Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel after Page Six contact

The controversy around Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel took another sharp turn this week. A new report has now raised questions about how both handled the fallout after their photos from their Arizona resort surfaced. The issue is no longer just about the images. It is now about what happened behind the scenes.Russini, a senior NFL insider at The Athletic until this week, stepped down amid growing scrutiny. The development followed a detailed report by ESPN’s Ben Strauss on April 17, 2026, which pointed to a coordinated response effort between Russini and Vrabel after Page Six first approached them.According to Strauss’ report, Russini contacted a crisis communications expert soon after a New York Post reporter reached out with the photos. The same report stated she also coordinated with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel on how to respond before the story went public. That detail has now become central to the controversy. Also, a viral post ignited the already heated firestormThe viral post reads, “Russini and Vrabel coordinated their crisis response. Neither could prove (to the Post or the Athletic) their initial claims that they were on separate trips with friends. Athletic asked Russini for texts or “photos proving her girls hiking trip claim-- nope”The Page Six report showed Russini and Vrabel at an Arizona resort appearing close, including moments when they held hands. Both denied any romantic involvement and claimed they were part of a larger group. However, questions began to grow when no supporting evidence surfaced.Executives at The Athletic asked Russini to provide proof. This included texts, travel details, or photos from the trip that could confirm the presence of others. According to the ESPN report, none of that evidence was submitted. That gap added pressure internally.Before the story broke, Russini also made her case to Meredith Kopit Levien, CEO of the New York Times Company, as ESPN reported on April 17. She described the situation as a “sexist attack on a female reporter in a male-dominated field.” The Athletic initially backed her. But that stance shifted as the review process began.Russini addressed the situation in her resignation letter posted on April 16, 2026. “Rather than allowing this to continue, I have decided to step aside now,” she wrote, adding she did not want to “lend it further oxygen.”The internal investigation by The Athletic is still ongoing despite her exit. Meanwhile, Vrabel, now head coach of the New England Patriots, has not issued a public statement.With no clear evidence and new details emerging, the story has moved beyond speculation. It now centres on credibility, response strategy, and unanswered questions.
Article Summary: New report points to coordinated response from Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel after Page Six contacted them. Russini, a senior NFL insider at The Athletic until this week, stepped down amid growing scrutiny. Both denied any romantic involvement and claimed they were part of a larger group.

Article Title: Is Will Anderson Jr. worth $150 million? Texans’ historic extension raises eyebrows despite his rising defensive dominance
Article Text:
Texans defensive end Will Anderson Jr. was rewarded with a three-year, $150 million extension. (Imagn Images)

Why did the Texans make Will Anderson Jr. the highest-paid non-quarterback?



The Houston Texans have moved quickly to secure one of the league’s most disruptive defenders, agreeing to a record-setting extension with Will Anderson Jr. after weeks of quiet anticipation. The deal, finalized Friday, keeps the 24-year-old edge rusher in Houston through his prime and signals a clear organizational direction. Anderson’s rise has been swift and emphatic, backed by elite production and postseason relevance. Now, with a historic payday in place, the Texans are doubling down on a belief that defense can still anchor a contender in today’s NFL.Houston didn’t wait for leverage to shift. Will Anderson Jr’s résumé already justified urgency. Through three seasons, he has delivered 30 sacks, two Pro Bowl selections, a Defensive Rookie of the Year award, and a first-team All-Pro nod. Last season alone, he set career highs with 54 tackles, 12 sacks, and multiple takeaways, finishing just behind Myles Garrett in Defensive Player of the Year voting.The numbers explain the contract, but the structure tells a deeper story. “The #Texans and All-Pro edge Will Anderson Jr.have agreed on a blockbuster, 3-year, $150M extension with $134M guaranteed to make him the NFL’s highest paid non-QB ever,” NFL Network’s Ian Rapoport reported on X on April 17. “The deal, which includes a rare no-trade clause, was done by @AgentNicoleLynn of @KlutchSports.” The agreement reportedly includes a $32 million signing bonus and $100 million fully guaranteed, reinforcing how central Anderson has become to the franchise.This isn’t an isolated move. General manager Nick Caserio has spent the past year investing heavily in defense, also committing significant money to Danielle Hunter and Derek Stingley Jr. The pattern is clear. Houston isn’t just building a good defense. It is building an expensive one, designed to control games.What stands out is the contrast on the other side of the ball. Quarterback C.J. Stroud remains on his rookie deal after an uneven 2025, and the offense, outside of Nico Collins, carries relatively modest financial weight. Even the offensive line’s combined cap hit sits below what many teams pay a single elite tackle. It’s a calculated imbalance.The risk, of course, is familiar. Recent playoff exits have exposed the gap. Despite strong defensive showings, turnovers and inconsistent quarterback play have led to decisive losses, including a 28–16 defeat in Foxborough marked by five giveaways. Houston’s defense has held up. The offense has not always followed.History offers mixed encouragement. Great defenses have carried teams to titles, but rarely sustained dominance. That reality makes Houston’s approach both bold and fragile.Still, the Texans appear comfortable with the bet. With Stroud’s extension decision looming in the next two years, the current window allows flexibility to keep adding defensive talent while searching for offensive answers through the draft. Recent picks suggest that effort is already underway.Houston isn’t following the modern blueprint. It’s challenging it. And with Anderson now locked in, the franchise has made its stance unmistakably clear.
Article Summary: Will Anderson Jr. was rewarded with a three-year, $150 million extension. The deal, finalized Friday, keeps the 24-year-old edge rusher in Houston through his prime. Anderson’s rise has been swift and emphatic, backed by elite production and postseason relevance.

Article Title: Cardi B And Stefon Diggs: “That’s why Stefon left her.”: Offset allegedly spills harsh reason behind Stefon Diggs and Cardi B's split amid shocking DNA test drama
Article Text:
Offset allegedly spills harsh reason behind Stefon Diggs and Cardi B's split amid shocking DNA test drama (Getty Images)

Offset allegedly revealed the reason why Stefon Diggs broke up with Cardi B amid the DNA test drama

The noise around rappers Cardi B and former Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs did not start with a single viral clip. It traces back to Cardi B’s divorce from Offset, where custody and paternity quietly became part of the legal fight. Court records show Offset pushed for a DNA test related to one of Cardi B’s children. The judge denied testing for a newborn but allowed it for another child, adding an early layer of tension.Around the same time, Cardi B’s rumored link with Stefon Diggs started gaining attention. The timelines overlapped just enough for the internet to connect the dots. What began as speculation quickly became a full-blown narrative, even before anything was publicly confirmed.The story took a sharp turn when a short clip, believed to be from an Offset livestream, surfaced online. In that video, he allegedly said, “That’s why Stefon left her, he knows that’s my baby. She’s scared to do the DNA test, she knows the real. I’m going for custody, I’m telling you right now.” The tone felt raw, and, given the earlier court filings, many saw it as an extension of an ongoing dispute rather than a random outburst.However, things did not stop there. Another viral element pushed the story even further. An Instagram quote attributed to Stefon Diggs began circulating widely. It read, “Imagine loving a child with everything in you, waking up every day for them, sacrificing for them… then finding out none of it was even real. I feel broken, disrespected, and completely fooled.” The words felt personal and emotional, which made them easy to believe.But that is where the narrative starts to fall apart. There is no verified source linking that quote to Stefon Diggs. No official post, no interview, no confirmed statement backs it. In fact, there is also no confirmed DNA test involving Cardi B and Diggs, despite how strongly the rumor has spread.What is confirmed is limited but important. Offset raised paternity questions during the divorce. The court did rule on those requests. At the same time, the legal order also restricts both Cardi B and Offset from making public remarks against each other. That raises serious questions about the viral clip itself.Meanwhile, Stefon Diggs has remained silent throughout. No response has come from his side. Still, a few days before the DNA chatter took over, the focus had shifted to a possible reunion between the rapper and the WR. The two were reportedly seen in the same social circles in Washington, D.C., including at a live event where Diggs was present to support Cardi B during a performance appearance.That sighting quickly sparked speculation that the duo might have patched up. Fans pointed to their presence at the same venue, along with overlapping timelines, as signs of a quiet patch-up. However, neither Cardi B nor Stefon Diggs addressed the rumors, leaving the reunion talk unconfirmed just before the DNA controversy took over the narrative.Right now, the story exists in two layers. One is real and documented through court filings. The other is built online through clips, quotes, and assumptions. And the gap between those two is where the confusion continues to grow.
Article Summary: The story around Cardi B and Stefon Diggs started with a viral clip. Court records show Offset pushed for a DNA test related to one of Cardi's children. The judge denied testing for a newborn but allowed it for another child. A short clip believed to be from an Offset livestream surfaced online.

Article Title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce hit unexpected wedding snag as guest list drama creates awkward personal dilemmas
Article Text:
travis kelce taylor swift (Getty Images)

Why is Blake Lively’s expected invite putting Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce in a difficult spot



As Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce move closer to their reported July 3 wedding in New York City, a private complication has surfaced around a very public question. The couple, known for tightly controlling details of their relationship, now face a delicate call over their guest list. At the center of it sits a once-trusted name, Blake Lively, whose history with Swift makes the decision far from routine and increasingly difficult to ignore.The tension traces back to a friendship that once felt unshakable. Taylor Swift and Blake Lively grew close after meeting in 2015, building a bond that extended beyond casual celebrity circles. Swift became godmother to Lively’s children with Ryan Reynolds, and even trusted her creatively, handing her the director’s role for the “I Bet You Think About Me” music video in 2021. When Swift’s relationship with Kelce became public in 2023, Lively was often seen alongside her at Kansas City games, cheering from the stands.That shared history now complicates what should be a straightforward wedding list. The relationship reportedly cooled in May 2025 after Swift was pulled into a legal situation involving Justin Baldoni. According to reports, a subpoena connected to a lawsuit filed by Lively created friction, with Swift unhappy about being drawn into the dispute.Since then, communication between the two has seemingly stalled.Yet, Lively appears unfazed. According to entertainment insider Rob Shuter, she remains confident about her place at the wedding. “Blake is totally optimistic,” an exclusive source told Shuter. “She’s acting like the invite is already in the mail. She keeps going back to the beginning. She was around when Taylor and Travis first got together. She feels like part of their story.”That confidence contrasts with the silence from Swift’s side. While “save the date” notices have reportedly gone out, there is no indication Lively has received one. Even so, her belief has not shifted. “There’s a lot of baggage right now,” another source said. “And Taylor is being incredibly careful about her guest list.”For Swift and Kelce, the issue is no longer just personal. It has become a balancing act between past loyalty and present boundaries. Ignoring Lively risks reopening old wounds. Including her could invite fresh scrutiny. With time running short, the couple may soon have to make a decision they had hoped to avoid.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly getting married on July 3. The couple has been tight-lipped about their guest list. Blake Lively is expected to be on the guest list for the wedding. Lively and Swift grew close after meeting in 2015. The relationship reportedly cooled in May 2025.

Article Title: Nfl Trade Rumors: NFL Trade Rumors: Kansas City Chiefs target crucial defensive upgrade to fix secondary ahead of 2026 Draft
Article Text:
Kansas City Chiefs target crucial defensive upgrade to fix secondary ahead of 2026 NFL Draft (Getty)

NFL trade rumors : Ephesians Prysock emerges as a perfect fit as the Kansas City Chiefs quietly explore a depth option to fix defensive cracks

The Kansas City Chiefs are back in the spotlight as trade rumors heat up ahead of the 2026 NFL draft, with Washington cornerback Ephesians Prysock now entering the conversation. The franchise, known for aggressive roster-building, appears to be looking deeper into the draft pool for answers.With clear gaps in the secondary after a turbulent offseason, Kansas City’s interest in a late-round defensive option could signal a calculated move. It is not flashy, but it could be necessaryThe latest trade chatter links the Kansas City Chiefs to cornerback Ephesians Prysock as a potential Day 3 target. A post in NFL circles captured the buzz: “The #Chiefs have interest in Washington CB Ephesians Prysock. Prysock is projected to go early day 3 of this year’s draft. The corner recorded 48 tackles, eight pass deflections, and one interception in 13 starts last season.”That interest lines up with what the roster currently lacks. In one stretch this offseason, the Chiefs lost Trent McDuffie, Jaylen Watson, and safety Bryan Cook. That group formed the core of their secondary. Replacing that production will not come from a single pick.Prysock brings a different look. He stands 6-foot-3 with long arms, which gives him an edge in contested coverage.He played over 700 snaps in each of the last three seasons in college, showing durability and consistency. His coverage numbers improved, too. He cut down yards allowed and finished his final season allowing just 8.4 yards per reception.That matters in Kansas City’s system under Steve Spagnuolo. The defense leans on zone concepts and physical press looks. Corners must read plays quickly and close space fast. Prysock’s length helps him disrupt passing lanes, especially in Cover 3 and Cover 4 schemes.There is also a bigger picture. Kansas City still has to address its pass rush. Losing key edge depth has created another hole. That likely pushes the front office to use an early pick on a pass rusher or safety. As a result, cornerback becomes a later priority. That is where Prysock fits perfectly.Of course, he is not a finished product. He tends to get handsy when beaten, which led to penalties in college. His height also forces him to play high at times, which quicker receivers can exploit. These are coachable issues, but they explain his Day 3 projection.Even so, this is how contenders stay competitive. They find value where others hesitate. For the Chiefs, adding a player like Prysock could quietly stabilize a shaken secondary while bigger moves address the top of the roster.
Article Summary: Washington cornerback Ephesians Prysock is a potential Day 3 target for the Kansas City Chiefs. The Chiefs lost Trent McDuffie, Jaylen Watson, and safety Bryan Cook this offseason. The franchise, known for aggressive roster-building, appears to be looking deeper into the draft pool.

Article Title: Who is Hollie Ford, Braxton Berrios’ new mystery blonde love interest following split with Alix Earle
Article Text:
Braxton Berrios and Hollie Ford (affinitypicture / BACKGRID)

What do Braxton Berrios and Hollie Ford’s latest outing reveal



Braxton Berrios appears to be moving on, and not quietly. The NFL free agent was seen in Los Angeles this week with model Hollie Ford, a sighting that quickly stirred speculation about his personal life just months after his split from Alix Earle. The timing matters. Berrios and Earle ended their two-year relationship in December 2025, with both sides describing it as mutual. Now, four months later, the wide receiver is back in the spotlight, this time for who he’s spending time with off the field.The two were spotted together on April 15 at Bird Streets Club, a private members-only spot in Los Angeles known for its low-key celebrity traffic. They arrived separately but left together, a detail that rarely goes unnoticed in these circles. Photos captured Berrios behind the wheel as they pulled away, though it was his small gestures that drew attention. He was seen opening the passenger door for Ford before they got in, a moment that suggested familiarity rather than coincidence.Earlier in the day, they were also seen sitting together over drinks at what looked like a café, chatting in a more relaxed setting. That contrast stood out. Their daytime appearance felt casual, almost unguarded, while the evening outing had a more deliberate tone, with both changing into sharper looks.Ford, 25, kept things simple but polished. She wore a white sleeveless blouse with dark blue jeans and minimal accessories, letting her natural style do most of the work. Her look leaned understated, but intentional. Berrios, now 30 and recently signed to a one-year deal with the Houston Texans, matched that energy with a light crewneck sweater, pinstriped pants, and sneakers. It was clean, composed, and noticeably relaxed.Ford isn’t new to attention. The London-based model has built a steady presence in fashion, working with brands like L’Oréal and ASOS while maintaining a curated social media profile. Her recent birthday post captured a glimpse of her tone online: “Another year around the sun 🎂✨so grateful xxx.” It’s light, personal, and in line with the image she projects publicly.For Berrios, the timing adds another layer. His name had already been circling headlines tied to Earle’s ongoing online tension with Alex Cooper . This sighting shifts the conversation. Whether it’s the start of something serious or just two people spending time together isn’t clear yet. But in a space where optics matter, this wasn’t a low-profile appearance.The bigger takeaway is simple. Berrios isn’t retreating from attention after his breakup. If anything, he seems comfortable letting his next chapter play out in plain view.
Article Summary: Braxton Berrios was seen with model Hollie Ford in Los Angeles. The sighting comes just months after his split from Alix Earle. The NFL free agent and the model were seen together at a private members-only club. They arrived separately but left together, a detail rarely goes unnoticed.
